name: Keeper
tagline: Best for secure file storage and business use
description: Keeper offers enterprise-grade security with excellent encrypted file storage, making it ideal for business users and those needing document protection.
longDescription: Keeper distinguishes itself with a strong focus on secure file storage and enterprise features. The encrypted vault supports not just passwords but also files, photos, and documents — up to 100GB on business plans. Security is top-notch: Keeper is FedRAMP and SOC 2 certified, making it one of the few password managers approved for US government use. The zero-knowledge architecture ensures even Keeper cannot access your data. For families and businesses, Keeper offers excellent sharing controls, activity reporting, and compliance tools.

keyFeatures.name: Encrypted File Storage
keyFeatures.description: Store up to 100GB of encrypted files alongside passwords. Supports documents, photos, and videos. Perfect for storing sensitive documents like tax returns.

keyFeatures.name: BreachWatch
keyFeatures.description: Continuously scans the dark web for your credentials. Alerts you in real-time if any of your stored passwords appear in known data breaches.

keyFeatures.name: KeeperChat
keyFeatures.description: Separate encrypted messaging app with self-destructing messages and secure file sharing. Included with Keeper Unlimited bundle.

keyFeatures.name: Admin Console
keyFeatures.description: Comprehensive admin dashboard for business plans. Manage users, enforce policies, monitor activity, and generate compliance reports.

verdict: Keeper is the best choice for business users and anyone who needs secure file storage alongside password management. The FedRAMP certification and enterprise-grade security make it ideal for organizations with compliance requirements. Individual users can find better value elsewhere, but for business use, Keeper is top-tier.

faqs.question: Is Keeper secure enough for business use?
faqs.answer: Yes, Keeper is one of the few password managers with FedRAMP Authorization, meaning it meets strict US government security standards. It also holds SOC 2 Type II and ISO 27001 certifications. The zero-knowledge architecture ensures even Keeper employees cannot access your data.

faqs.question: Does Keeper offer a free version?
faqs.answer: Keeper does not offer a permanent free plan, but they provide a 30-day free trial of the paid plans. Individual plans start at $2.92/month billed annually. For a free password manager, consider Bitwarden.

author.name: Alex Chen
author.title: Senior Cybersecurity Analyst
author.bio: Alex has tested over 30 password managers across 5 years.

seoMeta.title: Keeper Review 2026: 8.9/10 Rating | TrueTopReviews
seoMeta.description: In-depth Keeper review covering file storage, enterprise security, and pricing. Rated 8.9/10. Best for business.
